summary: This guest post offers a psychological interpretation of why Huma Abedin continues to support Anthony Weiner's mayoral campaign despite his sexting scandals. The author argues it's entirely about protecting their young son Jordan from future stigma. By reframing Weiner as 'almost mayor' rather than 'disgraced congressman,' Huma hopes to give Jordan a less humiliating narrative about his father. The post suggests the marriage is effectively over, but Huma is trapped by the permanent reality that Weiner will always be Jordan's father. The author concludes with sympathy for both mother and child while withholding her own vote.
key_points: Huma's support for Weiner's campaign is a strategic move to protect her son's future identity, not about saving the marriage
The marriage is effectively over, but divorce doesn't solve the permanent problem of Jordan's paternity
A 'Mayor Weiner' narrative is far better for a child than 'disgraced sexting congressman Weiner'
The best Huma can hope for is that her son's father becomes known for policies rather than penis jokes
Even sympathetic observers won't reward this calculated damage control with their votes
money_quotes: She's got a child for a husband, with a weird sexual addiction that on the positive side, doesn't appear to carry the threat of STDs.
The marriage is over. What she cares about is Jordan. And this is where she's really fucked.
Whatever happens, Anthony will always be her child's father.
You've got a child you love more than anything in the world, will sacrifice anything for, and will always now be stigmatized as the son of a celebrity-sized asshole.
The best scenario for Huma is if Anthony becomes mayor. Then she can divorce his ass, get primary custody and protect her child from growing up listening to penis jokes about his loser father.
And with a whole lot of luck, they might even be about how his father's penis was a lot smaller in the mind of the public than his policies.
key_insight: A political spouse's public loyalty during scandal may have nothing to do with the marriage and everything to do with damage control for the children who will inherit the narrative.
